care.summary: A sculptural succulent with round, pointed leaves that grow in a fan shape.
care.light: Bright indirect to direct light; tolerates low light but grows slower
care.water: Water sparingly; allow soil to dry completely between waterings. Every 2-4 weeks in summer, monthly in winter
care.soil: Very well-draining cactus/succulent mix with coarse sand or perlite
care.humidity: Low; thrives in dry indoor conditions
care.fertilizer: Dilute succulent or cactus fertilizer once in spring and once in summer
care.toxicity: Mildly toxic if ingested; keep away from pets
common_issues: Root rot from overwatering — the most common problem
Mushy leaf bases from excess moisture
Brown leaf tips from inconsistent watering
tips: Water only when the soil is bone dry — a moisture meter helps
Use a tall, heavy pot to prevent tipping as leaves grow tall
Propagate by leaf cuttings or division of offsets
Braided or curled leaves are achieved by training young leaves with ties
